### Post by hardyn on 2007-04-28
do the wobbly windows work?

this was a bug in beta, it was supposed to be fixed, i guess it didn't.
there were two command line switches that had to be "flipped" you should be able to find previous posts about this.

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=426127&highlight=compiz+no+cube](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=426127&highlight=compiz+no+cube)

If the worbbly windows work, it isnt a video card problem.

### Post by hardyn on 2007-04-28
didn't read your xorg.file

you MUST install the nvida-glx or nvidia-glx-new video drivers!!!!

there are many many posts on how to do this, feisty should allow you to do this automatically through the restricted software manager.

good luck.

### Post by fwojciec on 2007-04-28
sudo nvidia-xconfig --add-argb-glx-visuals

it'll add
Option		"AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
to the Screen section in your xorg.conf - which is missing at the moment

Restart X

---

### Post by fwojciec on 2007-04-28
Oh wait... you're still using "nv" driver - that's no good, you need to install the proprietary nvidia driver for Beryl to work.  Check the wiki on beryl-project.org - they have a pretty clear howto there.

### Post by fwojciec on 2007-04-28
In order to use nvidia driver it needs to be installed first of all.

> sudo apt-get install nvidia-glx

### Post by fwojciec on 2007-04-28
OK, there are two possibilites:

1.  This is not the right driver for your nvidia card, in which case you should do 

> sudo apt-get install nvidia-glx-new 

This will replace the nvidia-glx driver with nvidia-glx-new driver - you can revert back to the nvidia-glx driver by installing it afterwards (which will remove nvidia-glx-new).

2. Your xorg.conf is somehow misconfigured, but try the no 1. and see if  that helps, if not we'll go through no. 2

---

root@garymc-desktop:/home/garymc# sudo apt-get install nvidia-glx-new

Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
E: Couldn't find package nvidia-glx-new
root@garymc-desktop:/home/garymc#

### Post by fwojciec on 2007-04-28
Right, sorry, I didn't realize you were using Edgy (all this is somewhat easier in Feisty)

First try this:

> sudo apt-get install nvidia-kernel-common

This will be probably also already installed, but just in case it is not.

Secondy:

> sudo nvidia-xconfig

This will attempt to configure your xorg.conf automatically for your nvidia card/driver.

If neither of these things works and your nvidia driver is still not working there is a third possibility, but this gets more complicated.  One way that always works on my computer whenever nvidia driver is acting up is to do boot up with "nvidia" driver enabled, let it fail and drop you to shell, then, as root, type:

> dpkg-reconfigure xorg-xserver

Let me explain - this command is going to run X server configuration routine, it'll ask you a bunch of questions about your input devices, monitor, etc. and it'll create new xorg.conf file for you.  This should make the nvidia driver work, but it'll also set all the settings to default, so if you configured your monitor resolution, refresh rate, mouse buttons or anything of this sort these settings will be erased and you'll need to do all the manual configuring yourself.

Another option is to install the driver from nVidia website manually, but I can't help you with this, since I don't remember how to do it (I've done it once in the past, but it was a while ago).

Finally - check the following websites:
Ubuntu Edgy Guide ([link]("http://ubuntuguide.org/wiki/Ubuntu_Edgy#How_to_install_Graphics_Driver_.28NVIDIA.29")) - there are two different guides on how to install nvidia drivers there, there is also an explanation on how to use Envy, which is a script which attempts to install drivers automatically for you.
Beryl-Project Wiki ([link]("http://wiki.beryl-project.org/wiki/Install_Beryl_on_Ubuntu_Edgy_with_nVidia#Installing_the_nVIDIA_Driver")) - another good guide on how to get Beryl to work on Edgy with nVidia.

Anyways, let me know how it goes.

---
